clusters.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
clusters.neutral_headline: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
bullet_summary: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said
The WHO said that some passengers aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship may have been infected with the deadly hantavirus through extremely rare person-to-person transmission
Spain's health ministry said the MV Hondius is expected to arrive within three to four days
A British crew member who became ill after a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean is to be medically evacuated, officials have said

items.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
items.standfirst: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said.
items.content: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said.

items.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.
items.content: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

items.title: What we know about the cruise hantavirus outbreak and its origins
items.standfirst: A rare form of hantavirus is believed to have spread person-to-person on board a cruise ship. Three people are dead, four more are ill and nearly 150 others are stranded aboard the ship, where they will stay potentially for months until they are cleared to leave. Tom Hanson has more.
items.content: A rare form of hantavirus is believed to have spread person-to-person on board a cruise ship. Three people are dead, four more are ill and nearly 150 others are stranded aboard the ship, where they will stay potentially for months until they are cleared to leave. Tom Hanson has more.

items.title: How hantavirus spreads: What to know about rare person-to-person transmission
items.standfirst: The WHO said that some passengers aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship may have been infected with the deadly hantavirus through extremely rare person-to-person transmission.
items.content: The WHO said that some passengers aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship may have been infected with the deadly hantavirus through extremely rare person-to-person transmission.

items.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak to sail to Canary Islands
items.standfirst: Spain's health ministry said the MV Hondius is expected to arrive within three to four days.
items.content: Spain's health ministry said the MV Hondius is expected to arrive within three to four days.

items.title: Unwell British crew member to be ‘urgently’ evacuated from cruise ship after hantavirus cases
items.standfirst: Briton and Dutch colleague to be removed from MV Hondius, as ship sets course for Canary Islands after deal struck with Spain What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member who became ill after a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean is to be medically evacuated, officials have said.The crew member is being prepared for medical evacuation from the MV Hondius along with a Dutch colleague, with Dutch authorities overseeing the operation. Continue reading...
items.content: Briton and Dutch colleague to be removed from MV Hondius, as ship sets course for Canary Islands after deal struck with Spain What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member who became ill after a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean is to be medically evacuated, officials have said.The crew member is being prepared for medical evacuation from the MV Hondius along with a Dutch colleague, with Dutch authorities overseeing the operation. items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship to arrive in Canary Islands in 'three to four days'
items.standfirst: Two critically ill crew from the MV Hondius will be evacuated via Cape Verde to the Netherlands after a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has killed three. The quarantined vessel, anchored off Praia, is expected to resume its journey to the Canary Islands once evacuations are complete.
items.content: Two critically ill crew from the MV Hondius will be evacuated via Cape Verde to the Netherlands after a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has killed three. The quarantined vessel, anchored off Praia, is expected to resume its journey to the Canary Islands once evacuations are complete.

items.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
items.standfirst: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.
items.content: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.(Image credit: Patrick Pleul/picture alliance)

items.title: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.
items.content: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

items.title: Cruise passengers tell of life on board stranded ship after hantavirus outbreak
items.standfirst: While passengers onboard the MV Hondius say the situation is calm, they face days at sea as officials warn the disease may have spread.
items.content: While passengers onboard the MV Hondius say the situation is calm, they face days at sea as officials warn the disease may have spread.

items.title: Race to find port for cruise ship as WHO traces how hantavirus spread
items.standfirst: Health authorities raced Tuesday to find a port for a cruise ship battling a hantavirus outbreak, as it remained off the West African coast with passengers isolating after three people died. Meanwhile, the World Health Organization said it suspects some rare human to human transmission of the virus took place between close contacts on board the ship.
items.content: Health authorities raced Tuesday to find a port for a cruise ship battling a hantavirus outbreak, as it remained off the West African coast with passengers isolating after three people died. Meanwhile, the World Health Organization said it suspects some rare human to human transmission of the virus took place between close contacts on board the ship.

items.title: Human-to-human hantavirus transmission suspected on board stranded cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: The cruise ship carrying almost 150 people at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak was marooned in the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, waiting for two ill crew members to be evacuated for treatment.
items.content: The cruise ship carrying almost 150 people at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak was marooned in the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, waiting for two ill crew members to be evacuated for treatment.

items.title: British crew member in need of urgent medical care amid suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
items.standfirst: WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who diedWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member was in need of urgent medical care and a passenger from the UK remained in a critical but stable condition following a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.Three people have died and medics on Monday were scrambling to evacuate two others from the MV Hondius, which set off in March from southern Argentina carrying 149 people from 23 countries. The crisis emerged late on Sunday after the World Health Organization (WHO) said it was investigating a suspected outbreak. Continue reading...
items.content: WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who diedWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member was in need of urgent medical care and a passenger from the UK remained in a critical but stable condition following a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.Three people have died and medics on Monday were scrambling to evacuate two others from the MV Hondius, which set off in March from southern Argentina carrying 149 people from 23 countries. The crisis emerged late on Sunday after the World Health Organization (WHO) said it was investigating a suspected outbreak. Continue reading...

items.title: Cruise ship at center of suspected deadly hantavirus outbreak refused permission to dock
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has been refused permission to dock, with nearly 150 people still on board — two of them seriously ill
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has been refused permission to dock, with nearly 150 people still on board — two of them seriously ill

clusters.title: Romania’s pro-EU coalition collapse 'does not alter the country's foreign policy', analyst says
clusters.neutral_headline: Romanian PM ousted in no-confidence vote
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Romania’s pro-EU coalition collapse 'does not alter the country's foreign policy', analyst says
items.standfirst: Romania's parliament on Tuesday voted to oust liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan in a motion of no confidence initiated by the Social Democrats and the far right, deepening political turmoil in the EU and NATO country bordering Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Laurențiu Pleșca, Policy Fellow at the German Marshall Fund of the United States. He says that the collapse of the government "does not alter Romania's foreign policy or security trajectory".
items.content: Romania's parliament on Tuesday voted to oust liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan in a motion of no confidence initiated by the Social Democrats and the far right, deepening political turmoil in the EU and NATO country bordering Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Laurențiu Pleșca, Policy Fellow at the German Marshall Fund of the United States. He says that the collapse of the government "does not alter Romania's foreign policy or security trajectory".

items.title: Romanian PM ousted in no-confidence vote
items.standfirst: Ilie Bolojan lost the vote after the largest party in his coalition joined the far-right opposition to depose him.
items.content: Ilie Bolojan lost the vote after the largest party in his coalition joined the far-right opposition to depose him.

items.title: Romania's pro-Europe government collapses after PM loses no-confidence vote
items.standfirst: Romania's pro-European coalition government has collapsed, after MPs voted against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, plunging the country into further political turmoil.
items.content: Romania's pro-European coalition government has collapsed, after MPs voted against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, plunging the country into further political turmoil.

clusters.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
clusters.neutral_headline: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
bullet_summary: The blast occurred in the city of Changsha, in Hunan province, on Monday afternoon, China’s official news agency Xinhua said
China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang

items.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
items.standfirst: Other fireworks manufacturers in Liuyang, in central Hunan province, ordered to halt production after deadly blastAn explosion at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has killed at least 26 people and injured 61, prompting the halting of all firework manufacturing near the site.The blast occurred in the city of Changsha, in Hunan province, on Monday afternoon, China’s official news agency Xinhua said. China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang. Continue reading...

China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang. Continue reading...

items.title: Voters cast ballots in Ohio and Indiana in high-stakes primaries for 2026 midterms – live
items.standfirst: Polls close in Indiana and soon in Ohio; control of Congress at stake as Senate seat in Ohio could tip scale for Democrats to retake chamberSign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump has issued a fresh verbal attack against Pope Leo XIV, accusing the pontiff of “endangering a lot of Catholics” because “he thinks it’s fine for Iran to have a nuclear weapon”.The remarks come two days before Marco Rubio, the US secretary of state, meets Leo at the Vatican in an effort to ease the tensions sparked by Trump’s previous broadside against the Chicago-born pontiff over his condemnation of the US-Israeli war on Iran. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump-Pope clash rehashes old gripes, casts cloud over Rubio's Vatican visit
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has intensified his criticism of Pope Leo XIV, who responded to the US president's broadside on Tuesday by reaffirming his mission to preach peace while accepting public scrutiny. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio sought to downplay the verbal clash Tuesday during a White House press briefing on Iran, just days before he visits the Vatican, raising concerns the dispute could hinder delicate diplomatic efforts.
items.content: Donald Trump has intensified his criticism of Pope Leo XIV, who responded to the US president's broadside on Tuesday by reaffirming his mission to preach peace while accepting public scrutiny. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio sought to downplay the verbal clash Tuesday during a White House press briefing on Iran, just days before he visits the Vatican, raising concerns the dispute could hinder delicate diplomatic efforts.

items.title: Arsenal beat Atletico to reach first Champions League final in 20 years
items.standfirst: Bukayo Saka seals a 1-0 win for Arsenal as they take their Champions League semifinal 2-1 on aggregate against Atletico.
items.content: Bukayo Saka seals a 1-0 win for Arsenal as they take their Champions League semifinal 2-1 on aggregate against Atletico.

items.title: Arsenal reach first Champions League final in 20 years after Saka goal beats Atletico
items.standfirst: Arsenal is back in the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years thanks to a late first-half goal from Bukayo Saka that secured a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid on Tuesday.
items.content: Arsenal is back in the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years thanks to a late first-half goal from Bukayo Saka that secured a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid on Tuesday.

items.title: Arsenal seek to end 20-year wait for Champions League final in showdown with Atletico
items.standfirst: Arsenal will be seeking to end a 20-year wait when they face off against Atletico Madrid Tuesday as they aim to reach the Champions League final for the first time since 2006. Mikel Arteta's side earned a 1-1 draw in the semi-final first leg in Madrid last week, putting them in position to finish the job at the Emirates Stadium.
items.content: Arsenal will be seeking to end a 20-year wait when they face off against Atletico Madrid Tuesday as they aim to reach the Champions League final for the first time since 2006. Mikel Arteta's side earned a 1-1 draw in the semi-final first leg in Madrid last week, putting them in position to finish the job at the Emirates Stadium.

clusters.title: Japanese PM Takaichi in push to revise pacifist constitution as regional tensions rise
clusters.neutral_headline: Japan sees largest protest in support of pacifist constitution...

items.title: Japanese PM Takaichi in push to revise pacifist constitution as regional tensions rise
items.standfirst: Due to its wartime past, Japan adopted a pacifist Constitution that limits its military to self-defence. Now Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is calling for 'advanced discussions' on revising the Constitution, setting up a panel tasked with reviewing the country's security and defence policies as regional tensions escalate. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Giulio Pugliese, Lecturer in Strategic Communications at King's College London and Director of the EU-Asia Project at the European University Institute.
items.content: Due to its wartime past, Japan adopted a pacifist Constitution that limits its military to self-defence. Now Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is calling for 'advanced discussions' on revising the Constitution, setting up a panel tasked with reviewing the country's security and defence policies as regional tensions escalate. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Giulio Pugliese, Lecturer in Strategic Communications at King's College London and Director of the EU-Asia Project at the European University Institute.

items.title: Japan sees largest protest in support of pacifist constitution as PM Takaichi pushes revisions
items.standfirst: Japanese leader Sanae Takaichi has called for discussions to revise the constitution, saying it should ‘reflect the demands of the times’Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has called for “advanced discussions” on revising the pacifist constitution, as large demonstrations were held nationwide to oppose any changes to the country’s supreme law.Speaking during an official visit to Vietnam, Takaichi said the constitution, which was written by US occupation forces after the second world war, “should periodically be updated to reflect the demands of the times”. Continue reading...
items.content: Japanese leader Sanae Takaichi has called for discussions to revise the constitution, saying it should ‘reflect the demands of the times’Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has called for “advanced discussions” on revising the pacifist constitution, as large demonstrations were held nationwide to oppose any changes to the country’s supreme law.Speaking during an official visit to Vietnam, Takaichi said the constitution, which was written by US occupation forces after the second world war, “should periodically be updated to reflect the demands of the times”. Continue reading...

items.title: Narendra Modi’s BJP wins election in West Bengal for the first time
items.standfirst: Result in key Indian state is set to have significant implications for the country’s political landscapeNarendra Modi’s party has won a resounding election victory in West Bengal, a state which had been a rare opposition stronghold, expanding his unrivalled consolidation of power across the country.It is the first time that the Indian prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) has won assembly elections in West Bengal, a large and politically significant state in eastern India. Continue reading...
